name: Mahalia Jackson's Okra Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Cooked ham, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 1/2 lb Beef stew meat, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 lb Salt pork, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the blue crabs and discard any spongy substance in their shells. Reserve the claws and other meaty portions.
2. Clean the shrimp and reserve the shells. Place the shrimp shells in a deep saucepan with enough water to cover generously, then simmer for 30 minutes or longer to make broth.
3. Heat oil in a heavy skillet until it reaches a depth of 1/8 inch. Add beef, ham, sausage, gizzards, and salt pork to the hot oil. Sauté until lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
4. Pour the meat mixture into a large kettle. Add one can of tomatoes and enough strained shrimp broth to cover generously. Add bay leaves and simmer for about 30 minutes.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons more oil in another heavy skillet. Add onions, green peppers, celery, and garlic. Sauté until lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
6. Add the vegetable mixture and chicken parts to the kettle. Simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
7. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a separate heavy skillet. Add okra and cook, stirring often, until it turns lightly browned and loses its stickiness, about 30 minutes.
8. Add the shrimp to the okra and sauté for 3 or 4 minutes longer, or until the shrimp turns pink. Stir in 2 tablespoons of sugar.
9. Add the okra mixture to the kettle. Drain the remaining can of tomatoes, reserving the liquid. Add the drained tomatoes, crab, and enough tomato liquid and water from the shrimp shells to keep the mixture in the kettle soupy. Simmer for about 30 minutes.
10. Stir in parsley flakes and the remaining 2 tablespoons of sugar. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Serve immediately or refrigerate and reheat later. Serve over hot rice in deep soup bowls. Pass hot pepper sauce and crackers with gumbo, if desired.
